summary.tagline: An independent team project exploring water-quality prediction, external data enrichment, and leaderboard-driven model iteration.

method_stack.label: Core data
method_stack.detail: Official water quality training data, river coordinates, dates, Landsat, and TerraClimate features.

method_stack.label: External signals
method_stack.detail: HydroBASINS, terrain, soil, CHIRPS rainfall, NASA POWER, and weather proxies were explored as additional context.

method_stack.label: Model families
method_stack.detail: HGB, Extra Trees, KNN, graph smoothing, corridor logic, calibration layers, and target-wise blends.

method_stack.label: Validation discipline
method_stack.detail: Repeated offline ranking, branch tracking, leaderboard feedback, and round-by-round strategy resets.

milestones.title: Benchmark Start
milestones.detail: We started from the official benchmark and used it as the reproducibility anchor for every later round.

milestones.title: Early Lift
milestones.detail: Target-wise tuning and cleaner search discipline produced the first meaningful jump.

milestones.title: EC Signal Breakthrough
milestones.detail: Weather-enriched EC modeling became the first clear sign that external signals could help when used carefully.

milestones.title: Corridor Regime
milestones.detail: TA/EC link calibration and near-region corrections moved the project into a much stronger operating regime.

milestones.title: Stable TA Refinement
milestones.detail: TA-focused refinement became a reliable way to improve without sacrificing the rest of the stack.

milestones.title: Hydro Corridor Peak
milestones.detail: A hydro micro-corridor boundary push at 40/40 became the first confirmed frontier above the long 0.375 plateau.

milestones.title: Plateau Confirmed
milestones.detail: By round 42, safe pushes out to 60/60 still tied the same frontier, showing this family had become a stability baseline.

milestones.title: POWER Reset Failure
milestones.detail: The first NASA POWER attack regressed online, proving that broad direct replacement was not submission-safe.

milestones.title: Recovery via Positive-Only Guards
milestones.detail: Freezing EC and allowing only tiny positive TA deltas recovered safety and restored the frontier score.

milestones.title: Strong Attack Postmortem
milestones.detail: A direct-family strong attack failed hard, which became one of the most useful failure analyses in the project.

worked.title: Target-wise modeling
worked.detail: Separating TA, EC, and DRP decisions made the search much more controlled than one-size-fits-all modeling.

worked.title: External data with tight safety gates
worked.detail: Landsat, TerraClimate, CHIRPS, hydro, terrain, and weather signals helped only when injected conservatively.

worked.title: Risk-aware post-processing
worked.detail: Fallback logic, corridor boundaries, and replay anchors repeatedly prevented avoidable leaderboard collapses.

worked.title: Failure reviews as part of the loop
worked.detail: Major review rounds and postmortems turned bad submissions into better strategy, not just frustration.

failed.title: Direct full replacement with new families
failed.detail: Aggressive swaps with POWER/CHIRPS-style families caused large online regressions when calibration drift was too broad.

failed.title: Overly cautious micro-edits
failed.detail: Very safe DRP or tiny TA/EC edits often looked principled but could not move the leaderboard.

failed.title: Family changes without anchoring
failed.detail: Changing model families alone was rarely enough; structure and calibration had to come with it.

selected_notebooks.title: Official Benchmark Reference
selected_notebooks.summary: The starting point provided by the official challenge package.

selected_notebooks.title: Round 11 EC Guard Notebook
selected_notebooks.summary: A curated public notebook brief for the EC-focused stabilization phase.

selected_notebooks.title: Round 15 TA Refine Push
selected_notebooks.summary: A curated public notebook brief for the TA-focused refinement phase that pushed the score into the mid 0.36 range.

selected_notebooks.title: Round 18 DRP Ultralight Probe
selected_notebooks.summary: A curated public notebook brief for a careful DRP probe without destabilizing the full submission.

selected_notebooks.title: Round 20 Structural TA+EC Notebook
selected_notebooks.summary: A curated public notebook brief showing the move toward structural routing and corridor-aware adjustments.
